text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Regular consumption of nuts has been associated with a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease in several large prospective cohort studies.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Regular consumption of nuts has been associated with a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease in several large prospective cohort studies.","entailment":"The source explicitly states that regular nut consumption is linked to lower cardiovascular disease risk in large cohort studies.","population":"general population in large prospective cohort studies","intervention":"regular consumption of nuts","comparator":"","outcome":"risk of cardiovascular disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"In the Nurses' Health Study, women who ate nuts five or more times per week had a 35% lower risk of coronary heart disease than women who rarely ate nuts.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In the Nurses' Health Study, women who ate nuts five or more times per week had a 35% lower risk of coronary heart disease than women who rarely ate nuts.","entailment":"The source provides the specific comparative risk reduction for women eating nuts frequently versus rarely.","population":"women in the Nurses' Health Study","intervention":"ate nuts five or more times per week","comparator":"women who rarely ate nuts","outcome":"risk of coronary heart disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"35% lower risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"A 2013 randomized controlled trial (PREDIMED) found that a Mediterranean diet supplemented with mixed nuts reduced the incidence of major cardiovascular events by approximately 28% compared with a control diet.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A 2013 randomized controlled trial (PREDIMED) found that a Mediterranean diet supplemented with mixed nuts reduced the incidence of major cardiovascular events by approximately 28% compared with a control diet.","entailment":"The source reports the RCT result showing a ~28% reduction in major cardiovascular events with nut‑supplemented diet versus control.","population":"participants in the 2013 PREDIMED randomized controlled trial","intervention":"Mediterranean diet supplemented with mixed nuts","comparator":"control diet","outcome":"incidence of major cardiovascular events","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"approximately 28% reduction","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Observational findings may be confounded because nut consumers in these cohorts tended to exercise more, smoke less, and have higher incomes.","claimType":"methodological","quote":"Observational findings may be confounded: nut consumers in these cohorts tended to exercise more, smoke less, and have higher incomes.","entailment":"The source notes that the observed associations could be due to differences in lifestyle and socioeconomic factors among nut consumers.","population":"nut consumers in these cohorts","intervention":"nut consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"exercise more, smoke less, higher incomes (potential confounders)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Critics also note that the PREDIMED trial was partially re‑analysed in 2018 after irregularities in randomization at some sites.","claimType":"methodological","quote":"Critics also note that the PREDIMED trial was partially re‑analysed in 2018 after irregularities in randomization at some sites,","entailment":"The source indicates that the trial underwent a re‑analysis due to randomization issues, which is a methodological detail.","population":"PREDIMED trial","intervention":"partial re‑analysis in 2018 after randomization irregularities","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The corrected analysis reported similar
